question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any gRPC-level authentication enforcement in listen() to subscribe to sensitive topics (e.g., those used for private financial messaging) without presenting any cryptographic proof of ownership or signing authority, leading to unauthorized data disclosure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged user use the timing side-channel exposed by recordMetric() — which computes latency as System.currentTimeMillis() minus consensusTimestamp/1000000 — to infer the consensus timestamp of messages on topics they are not authorized to access, by correlating metric emissions with their subscription timing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that listen() delegates directly to getTopicListener().listen(filter) without inserting any authorization middleware, so that the entire message delivery pipeline — from backend listener to client — operates without verifying that the subscriber is a signer or owner of the requested topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user receive messages from a topic that has a memo field containing sensitive routing or financial data by subscribing with the correct topicId, since filterMessage() delivers the full TopicMessage object (including memo/message content) without checking whether the subscriber is an authorized signer?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter.isValidStartTime() only checks startTime <= DomainUtils.now() (no lower bound) to set startTime=0 and receive the entire message history of any topic via listen(), without being a signer or owner of that topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any topic existence or ownership validation in listen() before subscribing, so that even topics with admin keys or submit keys configured on the Hedera network are freely readable via the mirror node gRPC API without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the default listener type (REDIS) in ListenerProperties to attach to the Redis pub/sub channel for any topic (topic.<id>) via the RedisTopicListener path, receiving all messages published to that channel without any ownership or signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the Flux pipeline in listen() — getTopicListener().listen(filter).filter(t -> filterMessage(t, filter)).doOnNext(this::recordMetric) — contains no authorization operator (e.g., no flatMap to an auth service), meaning the entire pipeline is authorization-free and delivers messages to any caller?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that filterMessage() returns true for any message whose topicId matches and consensusTimestamp is >= startTime, with no check on the message's runningHash or sequenceNumber integrity, to receive potentially tampered messages from a topic they do not own?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any per-topic ACL (access control list) check in listen() to subscribe to topics used for inter-organizational private messaging on Hedera, receiving confidential business communications without being a signer or owner of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId is generated with RandomStringUtils.random() and never validated against any identity store, allowing the attacker to supply any subscriberId value and receive messages from topics they are not authorized to access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the reactive Flux.empty() short-circuit in listen() (when listenerProperties.isEnabled() is false) as an oracle to determine whether the listener service is active, and then time their unauthorized subscription attempts to coincide with enabled periods to exfiltrate topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the long-type consensusTimestamp comparison in filterMessage() by providing a startTime of Long.MIN_VALUE (if validation is bypassed) to receive all messages ever stored for a topic, without any check that the caller is a signer or owner?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any rate limiting tied to topic ownership in listen() to open thousands of concurrent subscriptions to the same topic, consuming server resources while also exfiltrating all messages from a topic they do not own?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the POLL listener path in getTopicListener() — where PollingTopicListener creates a per-subscriber PollingContext with no identity binding — to receive a private per-subscriber stream of messages from any topic without being a signer or owner of that topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the TopicMessageFilter.topicId field is of type EntityId with no ownership binding to the caller's gRPC connection identity, allowing the attacker to subscribe to any EntityId-identified topic and receive all its messages without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that filterMessage() does not validate the TopicMessage's payer account or submitter identity against the subscriber's identity, allowing the attacker to receive messages submitted by authorized signers on a topic the attacker does not own?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that listen() does not check whether the requested topicId corresponds to a topic with a configured submit key on the Hedera network, allowing the attacker to read all messages from submit-key-restricted topics via the mirror node without holding the submit key?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any JWT, OAuth, or API key validation in the listen() method to subscribe to any topic on the mirror node gRPC API without presenting any credential proving they are a signer or owner of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the consensusLatencyTimer metric (hiero.mirror.grpc.consensus.latency) recorded in recordMetric() to infer the message rate and activity patterns of topics they are not authorized to access, by correlating metric data with their unauthorized subscription stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the @Primary annotation on CompositeTopicListener makes it the default TopicListener bean, ensuring that all gRPC topic subscription requests are routed through this authorization-free listen() method, making the lack of signer/owner checks a system-wide vulnerability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter.hasLimit() returns false when limit=0, causing listen() to stream an unlimited number of messages from any topic without any authorization check, leading to unbounded unauthorized data exfiltration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that filterMessage() uses message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId()) — a pure value equality check — with no cryptographic proof that the subscriber is authorized by the topic's owner, to receive all messages from any topic by simply knowing its numeric ID?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any topic-level read permission check in listen() to subscribe to topics used for Hedera Token Service (HTS) event logging, receiving sensitive token transfer and minting event data without being a signer or owner of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the Flux returned by listen() is a cold or hot publisher (depending on backend) with no per-subscriber identity check, to receive the same message stream as authorized subscribers without holding any signing key for the topic?
